A Safer Alternative to git push --force: git push --force-with-lease

After using git rebase or git commit --amend to rewrite history, you have to force-push to update the remote. But git push --force overwrites the remote unconditionally — which can blow away your teammates' work. This post introduces a safer alternative: git push --force-with-lease and --force-if-includes, which add a layer of protection before you force-push.
